facts.text: Hutchinson CDP was named for Francis Asbury, the first American bishop of the Methodist Episcopal Church.

The Asbury Historic District, which surrounds Hutchinson, entered the National Register of Historic Places in 1993 for its architecture, industry, religion, community development, politics, and commerce.
Hoffman Grist Mill, a historic landmark within the district, now hosts programs by the Musconetcong Watershed Association that celebrate the area’s social, agricultural, and industrial heritage.
Along the Musconetcong River, the Plenge Archaeological Site in Hutchinson is one of only two major Paleo‑Indian sites in New Jersey, revealing the region’s ancient past.
County Route 632, locally called Asbury Anderson Road, runs east–west through Hutchinson and intersects County Route 643, Old Main Street, which runs north–south.
Hutchinson sits at 40.7737 degrees north latitude and 75.1313 degrees west longitude, placing it in Warren County’s Eastern time zone.
